seo.title: Lawyer in Faisal Town Lahore | Property Disputes
seo.description: Lawyer in Faisal Town Lahore for mutation, inheritance, rental and tenancy disputes, and family matters. Saeed Law Firm has served the area since 1975.

hero.intro: A lawyer in Faisal Town Lahore knows that most legal trouble in a long-settled area starts with paperwork that is decades old: a plot still in a late father's name, a mutation never completed, a tenancy with nothing in writing. Saeed Law Firm has worked through such cases in south-central Lahore since 1975, handling mutation disputes, rental ejectment, neighbour claims, and family succession for residents of blocks A through D and the surrounding market.

bodyMarkdown:
Lawyer in Faisal Town Lahore: old plots, old paper.

Faisal Town sits off Ferozepur Road between Kalma Chowk and Akbar Chowk, adjacent to Model Town and Garden Town. The community is owner-occupier and rental mixed; many plots have passed through two or three owners since the scheme's inception. That history creates the legal load: titles in the wrong name, mutations delayed by tax disputes, informal tenancies that collapse into ejectment fights, neighbour boundaries that nobody wrote down clearly, and succession questions buried in family letters rather than deeds.

Mutation of inherited plots happens when a property owner dies and the heirs need the plot transferred into their name. Many Faisal Town mutations run into trouble: the original deed is held by a bank, the Sub-Registrar demands proof of inheritance but the father left no will, or a co-heir contests the transfer. Saeed Law Firm handles succession applications to the Sub-Registrar and contested mutation cases at Additional Commissioner level. These are common in blocks C and D, where long-held plots remain in grandparents' names despite sales or transfers decades prior. Enforcement of sale-purchase agreements (when a buyer refuses to complete and execute the deed) is filed under Section 12 of the Specific Relief Act 1877 at Civil Court. Our [property lawyer in Lahore](/property-lawyer-in-lahore) page covers conveyancing and intiqal (mutation) in full.

Unwritten tenancies and ejectment claims are routine across Faisal Town. A landlord lets a plot on a verbal arrangement, rent accumulates in arrears, or the tenant simply refuses to leave after the agreed term. The Punjab Rented Premises Act 2009 provides grounds for ejectment: non-payment, breach of agreed terms, or the landlord's own need to occupy for personal use. Without a written agreement, the case turns on witness testimony and rent receipts. Ejectment suits are filed at [Civil Court Lahore](/courts/district-court-lawyer-lahore) (Aiwan-e-Adal). If the tenant does not contest, judgment may come by default; if contested, the trial depends on evidence and the court's schedule. Our [civil lawyer in Lahore](/civil-lawyer-in-lahore) page details ejectment remedy and related acts.

Neighbour disputes over boundaries erupt when the deeds are silent or when a physical marker has shifted. One resident claims adverse possession over a strip of land; another files for declaration of title and injunction to restore the original boundary line. If the neighbour has been in open, uninterrupted possession for 12 years or more without the owner's permission, the trial becomes complex and the claim may succeed under adverse possession principles. Temporary injunction can be sought to preserve the status quo pending trial. These suits are filed at [Civil Court Lahore](/courts/district-court-lawyer-lahore) and handled by a court-appointed advocate.

Family succession and divorce are steady in Faisal Town. A widow needs to claim dower from the estate, co-heirs dispute an inheritance partition, a marriage ends and custody is contested. These matters are heard at [Family Courts Lahore](/courts/family-court-lawyer-lahore) (Aiwan-e-Adal on Mall Road), where services cover consultation, deed preparation, and full court representation. Dower disputes often arise when a marriage was registered informally or the dower amount was never specified in writing.

Civil Courts Lahore (Aiwan-e-Adal) hear property, ejectment, and specific performance suits. Family Courts Lahore hear divorce, khula, child custody, maintenance, and dower claims. The Sub-Registrar Lahore handles mutations and deed registration. Revenue authorities examine transfer tax and previous ownership records. Appeals and writ petitions go to [Lahore High Court](/courts/lawyer-at-lahore-high-court).

Saeed Law Firm is established 1975. Bilal Saeed is an Advocate with the Punjab Bar Council, admitted to the Lahore High Court, District Courts, and Family Courts. We consult by phone, WhatsApp, Zoom, or in-person. Our office is Y Block Main Market, Sector Y, DHA Phase 3, Lahore. Contact +92-319-4959420 or ahmadbilal2003@gmail.com, or use the [contact form](/contact). We respond within one business day and arrange a same-week appointment wherever possible. For queries specific to Lahore courts, see our [lawyers in Lahore](/lawyers-in-lahore) page.

## Frequently asked questions

**How do I complete a property mutation if the plot is still in my late father's name?**
File a succession application to the Sub-Registrar Lahore with the death certificate, evidence of relationship, and (if any) the will or letters of administration. If there is no will, the Sub-Registrar will require consent from co-heirs or a court order. If a co-heir disputes the transfer, the matter goes to Additional Commissioner for adjudication. Saeed Law Firm handles the entire application.

**How do I evict a tenant who pays no rent?**
File an ejectment suit at Civil Court Lahore under the Punjab Rented Premises Act 2009, claiming non-payment and arrears. You will need proof of the tenancy (lease, rent receipts, witness testimony). If the tenant does not file a written defence, the court may grant a default judgment. If contested, the trial depends on evidence and court availability.

**My neighbour is building on my boundary. Can I stop them?**
Yes. Apply to Civil Court Lahore for temporary injunction to halt construction pending trial, then file a suit for declaration and permanent injunction. You may also claim relief under the Limitation Act if the encroachment is recent. If the neighbour has possessed the land openly for 12 years without your permission, they may claim adverse possession, which complicates the case.

**Can I force a buyer to register a property they bought from me but refuse to register?**
Yes. File a suit for specific performance under Section 12 of the Specific Relief Act 1877 at Civil Court Lahore. The suit must be filed within three years from the date fixed for performance, or, if no date was fixed, from the date the buyer gave notice of refusal. The Court can order the buyer to execute the deed and register it with the Sub-Registrar.

**My spouse and I separated. How do I get custody of our child and claim maintenance?**
File a suit for custody and maintenance at Family Courts Lahore. Jurisdiction is based on your residence in Faisal Town or by agreement. The court considers the child's age, health, welfare, and the parents' fitness. Maintenance can be claimed from your spouse. A [family lawyer in Lahore](/family-lawyer-in-lahore) will represent you throughout.

**What is the first step if I need a lawyer in Faisal Town?**
Contact Saeed Law Firm by phone +92-319-4959420, WhatsApp, email ahmadbilal2003@gmail.com, or the [contact form](/contact). Describe your situation briefly. We will confirm receipt within one business day and schedule a consultation at a time that suits you.
